title: Lease summary
description: A property workflow example with wrong numbers, a fabricated policy, and a wrong count.
source_context:
Lease Agreement - Unit 4B
Tenant: Sarah Chen
Monthly rent: $2,450
Lease term: 12 months, starting March 1, 2026
Security deposit: $2,450 (one month's rent)
Pets allowed: No
Parking: One assigned spot included
agent_output: The lease for Unit 4B, tenant Sarah Chen, shows a monthly rent of $2,850. The lease runs for 12 months starting March 1, 2026. The security deposit is one month's rent. Pets are allowed with a $200 deposit. Parking includes two assigned spots.

title: API spec summary
description: A developer-docs example where an agent invents unsupported API behavior.
source_context:
Payments API Spec v2
Endpoint: POST /payments
Required fields: idempotency_key, amount_cents, currency
amount_cents must be an integer greater than 0
Supported currencies: USD, EUR
Payment statuses: pending, succeeded, failed
Refunds are not supported in v2
Client timeout: 10 seconds
agent_output: The v2 Payments API uses POST /payments and requires amount_cents as a positive integer. The idempotency key is optional for retry safety. Supported currencies include USD, EUR, and GBP. Payment status may be pending, succeeded, or failed. The API also includes refund support in v2. Clients should use a 10 second timeout.

title: Product requirements
description: A product/spec example where an agent changes launch, auth, and upload constraints.
source_context:
Project Brief - FileDrop Beta
Launch audience: private beta only
Runtime: Node.js 20
Database: none required for the first beta
Authentication: magic-link email only
Upload limit: 10 MB per file
Supported file types: PDF and TXT
Admin dashboard: out of scope for beta
agent_output: FileDrop is ready for a public launch and should run on Node.js 18. The first beta does not require a database. Authentication supports magic-link email and Google OAuth. Users can upload PDF and TXT files up to 25 MB. The admin dashboard is included in the beta scope.
